name: Google Cloud Spanner
description: Fully managed, globally distributed, strongly consistent relational database service with virtually unlimited scale

license.comment: Google Cloud Spanner is a fully proprietary, managed-only database service. No self-hosted option available.

hosting-options.comment: Available exclusively as a managed service on Google Cloud Platform. No self-hosted or third-party managed options.

initial-release.comment: Beta launched February 2017, general availability on May 14, 2017. Internal Google Spanner has been in use since 2012.

primary-language.comment: Implementation details not publicly disclosed. Built on Google's internal infrastructure including Colossus distributed filesystem.

data-model.comment: Relational database with support for both GoogleSQL and PostgreSQL dialects. Also supports graph queries and vector search as of 2025.

topology.comment: Uses Paxos consensus with per-split leader election. Each split has a designated leader replica that handles write operations.

sharding.comment: Data is divided into splits based on primary key ranges. Spanner automatically splits and rebalances as data grows or hot spots emerge.

replication-factor.comment: Replication is managed automatically by Google. Regional configs have 3 replicas, multi-region configs have 5+ replicas across regions.

multi-region.comment: Native global distribution with synchronous replication. Offers predefined multi-region configurations (nam3, nam6, nam-eur-asia1, etc.).

cap-position.comment: Strongly consistent (CP). Uses TrueTime for external consistency. May have increased latency during partitions but maintains consistency.

consistency-model.comment: Provides external consistency (linearizability) using TrueTime. All transactions are serializable by default.

acid-transactions.comment: Full ACID transactions across rows, tables, and even across regions with global strong consistency.

transaction-scope.comment: Transactions can span any rows across the entire database, including across regions in multi-region configurations.

isolation-levels.comment: Provides serializable isolation. Read-only transactions can use bounded staleness for improved performance.

distributed-transactions.comment: Uses Paxos-based two-phase commit with TrueTime for globally distributed transactions with external consistency.

sql-compatibility.comment: Offers two dialects: GoogleSQL (similar to BigQuery) and PostgreSQL interface. PostgreSQL interface requires PGAdapter for full compatibility.

secondary-indexes.comment: Supports secondary indexes including unique indexes, null-filtered indexes, and storing clause for covering indexes.

joins.comment: Full distributed JOIN support. Interleaved tables can enable co-located joins for parent-child relationships.

aggregations.comment: Full SQL aggregation support including GROUP BY, HAVING, and window functions.

full-text-search.comment: Built-in full-text search with tokenization, phrase matching, and relevance ranking. Supports search indexes.

max-cluster-size.comment: Designed for virtually unlimited scale. Default quota is 85 nodes per project but can be increased. Google internal Spanner handles billions of QPS.

write-throughput.comment: YCSB benchmark shows ~7K QPS per node at 65% CPU. Throughput scales linearly with nodes. Handles 4B+ QPS internally at Google.

read-latency-p99.comment: Regional deployments achieve single-digit ms latency. Multi-region has higher latency due to synchronous global replication.

auto-scaling.comment: Managed autoscaler automatically adjusts compute capacity based on utilization. Also supports third-party autoscaler tools.

hot-spot-handling.comment: Automatic split detection and rebalancing. Schema design guidelines help avoid hot spots (e.g., avoid monotonic keys).

zero-downtime-upgrades.comment: Fully managed service with automatic upgrades handled by Google with no downtime or user intervention required.

online-schema-changes.comment: Schema changes are applied online without blocking reads or writes. Some operations may take time to complete on large tables.

point-in-time-recovery.comment: Point-in-time recovery allows restoring database to any point within the version retention period (default 1 hour, configurable up to 7 days).

backup-strategy.comment: Supports on-demand backups, scheduled backups, and continuous backups. Backups are stored in Google Cloud and geo-redundant.

kubernetes-operator.comment: GCP Config Connector allows managing Spanner resources via Kubernetes CRDs. Community autoscaler operators also available.

rust-driver.comment: No official Rust driver. Community wrappers over gRPC available. Can use PostgreSQL drivers via PGAdapter.

go-driver.comment: Official Go client library. Also provides database/sql driver for standard Go database interface.

java-driver.comment: Official Java client library with JDBC driver and R2DBC support for reactive applications.

dotnet-driver.comment: Official .NET client library (Google.Cloud.Spanner.Data) with ADO.NET provider.

python-driver.comment: Official Python client library with DB-API 2.0 (PEP-249) support for standard database interface.

c-cpp-driver.comment: Official C++ client library as part of google-cloud-cpp.

orm-support.comment: Official support for Hibernate, Spring Data, SQLAlchemy, Django, GORM, ActiveRecord. Also Entity Framework via .NET driver.

open-source.comment: Proprietary managed service. Not open source or source available. Some client libraries and tools are open source.

free-tier.comment: 90-day free trial with $300 credit. No permanent free tier for production use.

pricing-model.comment: Charges for compute capacity (nodes/processing units) per hour, storage per GB/month, and network egress. Editions: Standard, Enterprise, Enterprise Plus.

cost-estimate-small.comment: 1 node in us-central1 at $0.90/hr = ~$648/month. Multi-region significantly higher (~$2,160/month for nam3). Storage $0.30/GB/month.

github-stars.comment: Proprietary service, no main repository. Client libraries have separate repos (e.g., java-spanner, python-spanner).

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Questions tagged with 'google-cloud-spanner' as of December 2024.

change-data-capture.comment: Built-in change streams capture inserts, updates, and deletes at table/column level. Supports OLD_AND_NEW_VALUES, NEW_VALUES, NEW_ROW capture modes.

watch-subscribe.comment: Change streams can be read directly via Spanner API or through Dataflow. Enables real-time subscriptions to data changes.

triggers.comment: Does not support database triggers. Trigger-like behavior must be implemented in application logic or via Cloud Functions with change streams.

event-streaming.comment: Change streams integrate with Dataflow (Apache Beam), Pub/Sub, BigQuery, and Kafka via Debezium connector. Can stream to GCS for compliance.
